title: Crawl Graphs, Frontier Strategy, Visited State, Depth, and Determinism
description: URLs are marked visited after download, duplicates race into the queue, depth changes with concurrency, and restart cannot explain which parent discovered a page. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic crawl-frontier state machine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent pages, fetch identities, redirect edges, discovery edges, canonical hints, embedded resources, and rejection states separately.
Choose FIFO, depth-first, priority, sitemap, recrawl, or hybrid ordering from stakeholder value and fairness requirements.
Reserve an admitted URL atomically before scheduling and reconcile queued, active, completed, redirected, rejected, and retry states.
